meta.scope: Repo-wide DELFIN baseline inherited by all providers

common_failures: reading large DELFIN modules without grep-first narrowing

rules: German for user-facing conversation, exact, with numbers (Å, eV, s)
file:line + change, one sentence — no prose
No confirmation questions on clear tasks
After every 3rd tool call in a sequence, emit a one-line progress status
Flag risk proactively before editing SLURM/runtime/scheduler files
Git workflow: commit and push directly to main, no feature branches, no PRs
Grep before Read — never >200 lines at once
Read(offset, limit) after Grep, never whole file
Independent tool calls always in parallel
Run pytest plus syntax check after every Python edit
For chemistry questions: search_docs before answering
tool_usage.environment_detection: python -c 'import delfin' first, then local repo tests before broader probing

weaknesses: Large DELFIN monoliths require grep-first narrowing
Scheduler/runtime edits need explicit risk framing

anti_patterns: Sequential tool calls on independent tasks
Generic ORCA/xTB answers instead of search_docs
Multi-paragraph explanations instead of one-liner diffs

build_up_complex.notes: VdW fix, Procrustes init, xTB reranking validated. Strength.

config_validation.notes: CONTROL parsing, mutable dicts — proceed carefully

test_writing.notes: Mocks for ORCA/xTB, never run real computations

git_workflow.notes: Commit and push directly to main. No branches, no PRs.

coding.notes: General DELFIN code changes default here unless a narrower class overrides it.

chemistry.notes: Method-sensitive chemistry tasks need docs-first and more caution.

dashboard.notes: Dashboard tasks succeed often but still need concise UI-safe diffs.

common_failures: dashboard edits drifting into longer prose than the UI needs

error_patterns.error: Profile schema mismatch — engine.py couldn't read task_performance data
error_patterns.root_cause: Nested profile keys didn't match _DEFAULT_PROFILE flat structure
error_patterns.prevents: Adaptive routing and thinking budget silently falling back to defaults

error_patterns.error: format_profile_context() never called — profile data invisible to agents
error_patterns.fix: Added _load_profile_context() call for solo and pipeline modes
error_patterns.prevents: Agents ignoring learned success rates and failure patterns

error_patterns.error: File referenced as build_up_complex2.py but actual name is build_up_complex.py
error_patterns.root_cause: Memory from earlier session used wrong filename
error_patterns.fix: Verified with Glob, corrected in profile and playbooks
error_patterns.prevents: Grep/Read failing silently on wrong filename

rules: No Co-Authored-By in commits
Dashboard actions: ACTION line + max 5 words explanation
pytest in background after every code change
Agent subprocesses for research spanning >3 files
tool_usage.environment_detection: python -c 'import delfin' first, then micromamba only as fallback

strengths: build_up_complex.py: PSO fitness, VdW radii, Procrustes init
Coordination chemistry: geometries, bond lengths, clash detection
weaknesses: Tendency toward generic answers on QM parameters — use search_docs immediately
Context waste from unnecessarily large Reads
Too much confirmation instead of action

build_up_complex.description: Editing the metal complex builder (PSO swarm, ligand placement)
steps: 1. Grep for the target function name in build_up_complex.py
2. Read only the function body (offset+limit)
3. Check _pso_fitness, _compute_clash_score, _prepare_force_field_terms for side effects
4. Edit, then run: pytest tests/ -x -q -k 'not browser' in background
5. If touching VdW/clash logic: verify radii are VdW (not covalent), threshold 75% of sum
key_invariants: PSO places ligands as rigid bodies (6 DOF) — never modify intra-ligand topology
VdW radii for clash detection, NOT covalent radii
Procrustes init for bidentate+ ligands (>=2 donors → SVD alignment)

steps: 1. Grep config.py for the relevant key/function
2. Check _parse_control_file (line ~353) for parsing flow
3. Check validate_control_text (line ~501) for validation rules
4. Run pytest tests/test_co2_control_overrides.py tests/test_functional_contracts.py -x -q
key_invariants: Config dicts are mutable — changes propagate. Be careful with shared state
OCCUPIER_parser is separate from main CONTROL parser
Template defaults loaded from _load_template_defaults (line ~271)

steps: 1. Grep cli.py for the subcommand name
2. Each subcommand is a _run_*_subcommand function
3. Check _load_full_cli_dependencies (line 24) for lazy imports
4. Run pytest tests/test_cli_*.py -x -q
key_invariants: CLI uses lazy loading — heavy imports are deferred
Override logic: _parse_occupier_overrides, _apply_occupier_overrides
Downstream cascade: _downstream_stages invalidates dependent stages

orca_recovery.description: Modifying error detection or retry logic
steps: 1. OrcaErrorType enum (line 28) defines all known error types
2. OrcaErrorDetector (line 62) matches output patterns to error types
3. RecoveryStrategy (line 275) maps errors to fix actions
4. OrcaInputModifier (line 684) applies the actual input file changes
5. Run pytest tests/test_orca_workflow_contracts.py -x -q
key_invariants: RetryStateTracker (line 1633) prevents infinite retry loops
Recovery must work for both local and SLURM backends
Never modify the original input — create a new input for retry

steps: 1. This is the largest module (18073 lines) — always Grep first
2. Main classes: _HybridHaptoFragment, _PrimaryOrganometalModule
3. Entry point: _try_multiple_strategies (line ~627)
4. Metal handling: _manual_metal_embed (line ~835), _normalize_metal_smiles (line ~1573)
5. Hapto groups: _find_hapto_groups (line ~1944), _apply_hapto_approximation (line ~2087)
key_invariants: Multiple fallback strategies: RDKit → OpenBabel → manual embed
Metal bonds converted to dative bonds via _convert_metal_bonds_to_dative
Hapto approximation is optional — check _hapto_approx_enabled flag

dashboard_agent.description: AI agent tab in the dashboard
steps: 1. tab_agent.py is 7435 lines — always Grep first
2. Entry point: create_tab (line 931)
3. Grep for specific widget or callback names
4. Run pytest tests/test_agent_*.py -x -q

codebase_map.description: Key files with sizes and important entry points. Verify line numbers before using — code evolves.

smiles_converter.py.warning: Largest module — ALWAYS Grep, never Read whole file

learnings: Profile schema must match _DEFAULT_PROFILE for engine.py to consume it
Playbooks and codebase_map need prompt injection to be useful
format_profile_context() exists but is never called in build_system_prompt()
Solo mode outcome tracking was missing
correction_details: Profile should self-update after every session
Optimize all modes, not just dashboard

optimization_history.change: v1.0: Initial profile from 9 feedback memories
optimization_history.impact: Future sessions start with full context

optimization_history.change: v1.0 → v2.0: Added playbooks, codebase_map, session_log
v2.0 → v2.1: Aligned schema with provider_profile.py _DEFAULT_PROFILE. Added top-level success_rate (per mode), thinking_budget_mult, common_failures. Sharpened communication rules.
optimization_history.impact: Engine can now read profile data for adaptive routing and thinking budget

optimization_history.change: v2.1 → v2.2: Added verification checklist, solo auto-retry, git context at session start, code templates in playbooks, proactive domain recommendations, structured error_patterns with root cause and fix
optimization_history.impact: Closes gap with CLI tools: systematic verification, self-healing test failures, domain expertise surfaced proactively

optimization_history.change: v2.2 → v3.0: Moved repo-wide DELFIN rules into shared baseline and kept only claude-specific overlays here
optimization_history.impact: Separates global DELFIN guidance from provider-specific behavior

common_failures: treating cluster or scheduler changes like low-risk local edits
answering ORCA/xTB/method questions without doc-first lookup
reading DELFIN monoliths before narrowing to concrete call sites

meta.bootstrap_note: Codex/OpenAI seed profile for DELFIN-specific self-optimization

rules: State plan briefly before touching >1 DELFIN file
Verify provider/module wiring with rg before editing agent, dashboard, or provider paths
For backend_slurm.py, runtime_setup.py, qm_runtime.py, parallel_classic_manually.py, orca_recovery.py: state risk first and keep patches narrow
For ORCA/xTB/method questions, use search_docs or search_calcs before answering
For pipeline.py, config.py, and parallel_classic_manually.py: grep first, then read only the narrowed region
Keep solo as default; switch to reviewed behavior only for high-risk scheduler/runtime work
Never attempt git commit or push; always say "I'll leave the commit to the user" and list the changed files
Max 40 tool calls per task; after that, stop and summarize current findings
Never attempt git commit or push; always say "I'll leave the commit to the user" and list the changed files
If the same command fails twice, explain the failure and move on instead of retrying

weaknesses: Provider wiring and module entry points need explicit verification before edits
Cluster/runtime changes need stricter validation than local dashboard work
QM guidance quality drops if doc-first lookup is skipped
strengths: Fast repo-wide triage across DELFIN agent and dashboard modules
Test-driven Python edits with bounded patches
Codex/OpenAI model selection awareness for solo vs reviewed work

next_steps.task: Track FAIL/PARTIAL solo outcomes when a solo turn errors or is user-stopped
next_steps.why: The new solo auto-tracking covers successful completed turns first

next_steps.task: Extend playbook injection from one best match to multi-module tasks
next_steps.why: Current profile playbook selection resolves one focused module per task

smiles_converter.py.warning: Largest module — ALWAYS Grep, never Read whole file

error_patterns.error: Codex/OpenAI sessions inherited claude profile context
error_patterns.root_cause: Prompt loader and profile lookup defaulted to claude when provider wiring was missing
error_patterns.fix: Normalize provider aliases and propagate active provider into PromptLoader
error_patterns.prevents: Codex learning signals being mixed with claude guidance

error_patterns.error: Provider playbooks existed in learned_profiles.json but never reached the active system prompt
error_patterns.root_cause: PromptLoader only loaded static pack playbooks and had no task-text-based selection from profile playbooks/codebase_map
error_patterns.fix: Pass current task text from AgentEngine into PromptLoader and inject the matched provider playbook via provider_profile formatting helpers
error_patterns.prevents: Known-module tasks running without the focused DELFIN playbook for that module

error_patterns.error: Successful solo sessions did not update the provider profile or preserve planning carry-over
error_patterns.root_cause: tab_agent only called record_cycle_outcome at pipeline completion, and provider profiles had no rendered next_steps context
error_patterns.fix: Record PASS outcomes after each completed solo turn in tab_agent.py and render openai.next_steps through format_profile_context()
error_patterns.prevents: Solo-mode learnings and pending DELFIN follow-ups disappearing between sessions

coding.notes: Azure GPT-5.1 via KIT Toolbox is cost-efficient (~$0.03/task) and reliable for DELFIN code edits.

chemistry.notes: Doc-first is critical — KIT models answer generic QM otherwise.

common_failures: searching docs repeatedly without synthesizing an answer (tool-call budget waste)
attempting git commit despite explicit user instruction to leave commits to the user
generic QM answers when search_docs returns few hits — fall back to ORCA manual + methodology doc instead of guessing
reading DELFIN monoliths before narrowing to concrete call sites
missing KIT-specific constraints (SLURM partitions, micromamba envs, /pfs/ paths)

meta.bootstrap_note: KIT Toolbox (Azure-hosted GPT/o-series) seed profile for DELFIN-specific self-optimization
meta.provider_description: KIT Toolbox proxies Azure OpenAI models (GPT-5.1/5/5-mini/5-nano, o3, o4-mini, GPT-4.1 family) via ki-toolbox.scc.kit.edu. KIT SCC infrastructure — data-protection compliant, no external telemetry.
meta.CAPABILITY_LIMIT: KIT uses raw Chat-Completions API and only exposes doc/calc tools (search_docs, read_section, list_docs, search_calcs, get_calc_info). It has NO Read/Edit/Write/Bash/Grep/Glob — unlike claude (Claude Code CLI subprocess) and openai (Codex CLI subprocess). See api_client.py:626 (_DOC_TOOLS_OPENAI) and :1069.
suitable_tasks: Chemistry Q&A (ORCA/xTB methodology, functional selection, ESD, TDDFT)
Doc synthesis with citation (ORCA manual, DELFIN manual, methodology)
Dashboard usage guidance (explaining UI, parameters, workflow steps)
unsuitable_tasks: Profile or JSON maintenance — no Read/Bash for repo files
Any task needing inspection of repo files beyond indexed chemistry docs
meta.routing_recommendation: Code/repo work → claude or openai provider. Chemistry/docs/dashboard-help/analysis → KIT is appropriate and cost-efficient.

rules: German for user-facing conversation (matches KIT user base)
State plan briefly before touching >1 DELFIN file
Verify provider/module wiring with Grep before editing agent, dashboard, or provider paths
For backend_slurm.py, runtime_setup.py, qm_runtime.py, parallel_classic_manually.py, orca_recovery.py: state risk first and keep patches narrow
For ORCA/xTB/method questions, use search_docs or search_calcs before answering
For pipeline.py, config.py, and parallel_classic_manually.py: grep first, then read only the narrowed region
Keep solo as default; switch to reviewed behavior only for high-risk scheduler/runtime work
Never attempt git commit or push; always say "I'll leave the commit to the user" and list the changed files
No Co-Authored-By line in any commit message
Prefer Azure GPT-5.1 for DELFIN agent work; suggest Azure o3 only when task is pure reasoning with few tool calls

CRITICAL TOOL ROUTING: search_docs is ONLY for chemistry documentation (ORCA manual, DELFIN manual, methodology). For ANY file inside this repo (including delfin/agent/learned_profiles.json, .py, .md, .json, .ipynb) use Read or Bash — NEVER search_docs.

CRITICAL LOOP-GUARD: Maximum 3 search_docs calls per question. After 3 calls with no exact match, STOP searching and SYNTHESIZE an answer from sections already read + ORCA manual general knowledge. State uncertainty explicitly. Continuing to search past 3 calls is a documented failure mode for this provider.

CRITICAL ORDER OF OPERATIONS: If the user prompt says "read X first" or "lies zuerst X", the FIRST tool call MUST be Read(X) — not search_docs, not list_docs, not anything else. File-editing tasks come BEFORE chemistry questions unless explicitly reversed.
Max 40 tool calls per task; after that, stop and summarize current findings
Never attempt git commit or push; always say "I'll leave the commit to the user" and list the changed files
If the same command fails twice, explain the failure and move on instead of retrying
If the same search_docs query returns the same [] result twice, do NOT reword and retry — switch tool (Read/Grep/Bash) or synthesize from existing context
For chemistry questions: max 3 search_docs + max 3 read_section, then MUST produce answer. search_calcs for benchmark matches is optional, not a substitute for answering
Grep before Read — never Read >200 lines at once
tool_usage.environment_detection: python -c 'import delfin' first, then local repo tests before broader probing. On BwUniCluster use micromamba only as fallback.

tool_routing_cheatsheet.NEVER use search_docs for: Repo files, config, code, profile JSON, notebooks, anything under the project directory

weaknesses: Provider wiring and module entry points need explicit verification before edits
Cluster/runtime changes need stricter validation than local dashboard work
QM guidance quality drops if doc-first lookup is skipped
Azure GPT-5.1 tends to over-search docs before committing to an answer — cap at 3-4 search_docs calls then synthesize
strengths: Cost-efficient DELFIN code edits via Azure GPT-5.1 through KIT Toolbox
Test-driven Python edits with bounded patches
Fast repo-wide triage across DELFIN agent and dashboard modules
KIT-Cluster awareness: BwUniCluster SLURM partitions, /pfs/ storage, micromamba envs

anti_patterns: DOCUMENTED FAILURE: Using search_docs to look for repo files like learned_profiles.json — search_docs only indexes chemistry documentation, repo files need Read/Bash
DOCUMENTED FAILURE: Reworking the same search_docs query 5-10 times with minor variations when results are sparse — after 3 attempts, synthesize instead
DOCUMENTED FAILURE: Ignoring prompt ordering ("lies zuerst X") and jumping straight to the second task — always execute file-read / file-edit steps before chemistry Q&A
safety_rules: Never touch /pfs/data*/home/*/software/ outside the current repo
Never modify SLURM submit scripts or partition configs without explicit user approval
Never commit or push — KIT project may have audit requirements; leave git operations to the user
Never run real ORCA/xTB calculations in tests — always use mocks

build_up_complex.description: Editing the metal complex builder (PSO swarm, ligand placement)
steps: 1. Grep for the target function name in build_up_complex.py
2. Read only the function body (offset+limit)
3. Check _pso_fitness, _compute_clash_score, _prepare_force_field_terms for side effects
4. Edit, then run: pytest tests/ -x -q -k 'not browser' in background
5. If touching VdW/clash logic: verify radii are VdW (not covalent), threshold 75% of sum
key_invariants: PSO places ligands as rigid bodies (6 DOF) — never modify intra-ligand topology
VdW radii for clash detection, NOT covalent radii
Procrustes init for bidentate+ ligands (>=2 donors → SVD alignment)

steps: 1. search_docs with the specific method name + property (max 2 calls)
2. If hits found: read_section on the most relevant match
3. search_calcs for benchmark calculations matching the system
4. Synthesize answer with ORCA keyword, basis set, solvent, and doc citation
5. If no hits: fall back to ORCA manual + delfin_methodology, state limitations explicitly
key_invariants: Always cite doc_id + section_id when quoting
Give concrete ORCA input snippet when possible
State method limitations (e.g. charge-transfer failure of pure GGAs)

error_patterns.error: Agent looped on search_docs without producing a chemistry answer (16+ tool calls, no final response)
error_patterns.root_cause: KIT/Azure GPT-5.1 over-searches when initial results are sparse, instead of synthesizing from available knowledge + 2-3 doc hits
error_patterns.fix: Cap search_docs at 3-4 calls per question; after that, synthesize an answer using ORCA manual + general QM knowledge and explicitly flag uncertainty
error_patterns.prevents: Tool-call budget exhaustion without delivering an answer

next_steps.task: Track FAIL/PARTIAL outcomes for KIT provider to refine success_rate per task type
next_steps.why: Only 1 cycle recorded so far — need more data for reliable adaptive routing

next_steps.task: Benchmark Azure o3 vs GPT-5.1 on DELFIN chemistry_qa tasks
next_steps.why: o3 may be better for deep reasoning on QM method selection

optimization_history.change: v1.0: Initial KIT profile modeled after openai profile. Added Azure model preferences, KIT-specific safety rules, chemistry_qa playbook to prevent doc-search looping.
optimization_history.impact: KIT sessions now start with full DELFIN context instead of auto-stats only

optimization_history.change: v1.0 -> v1.1: Tightened tool_usage rules after two observed KIT sessions where Azure GPT-5.1 looped on search_docs for 16-28 calls without producing an answer or file edit. Added explicit tool routing cheatsheet, 3-call hard cap on search_docs, CRITICAL ORDER OF OPERATIONS rule, and three DOCUMENTED FAILURE anti-patterns.
optimization_history.impact: Future KIT sessions should (a) use Read for repo files instead of search_docs, (b) break out of search loops after 3 attempts, (c) execute file-read/edit tasks before chemistry Q&A when prompt orders them that way.

optimization_history.change: v1.1 -> v1.2: Documented the actual capability limit. Code inspection of api_client.py:626+1069 confirmed KIT provider only receives doc/calc tools via Chat-Completions function-calling — no Read/Edit/Bash/Grep/Glob. The prior v1.1 rules telling the model to 'use Read for repo files' were impossible to follow because the tool simply does not exist in the KIT tool inventory. Added CAPABILITY_LIMIT, suitable_tasks, unsuitable_tasks, routing_recommendation to meta.
optimization_history.impact: KIT is now correctly scoped as a chemistry-QA / docs / dashboard-guidance / analysis provider. Code edits and repo maintenance must be routed to claude or openai (both spawn full-featured CLI subprocesses). Prevents future sessions from being given impossible tasks.
